source.title: Land Wholesaling / Builder-First Land Arbitrage Model — YouTube Extraction
source.path: /root/Documents/Business Ideas Vault/Ideas/Land Wholesaling Builder-First Model - YouTube Extraction.md
source.notes: Transcript-mentioned states and markets plus deterministic seed scores for v1 weekly market scouting. Scores are research-prior inputs, not live proof.

criteria.label: New construction happening
criteria.threshold: At least five new builds in target ZIP / visible recent specs

criteria.label: More than five active builders nearby
Vacant/off-market lot supply
criteria.threshold: Enough unused residential lots to create seller call volume

criteria.label: Cookie-cutter repeatability
criteria.threshold: Similar lot sizes, conditions, roads/utilities, and pricing

criteria.label: 30–45 minute major-metro edge
criteria.threshold: Not fully built-out core, not isolated rural

criteria.label: Compliance / closing simplicity
criteria.threshold: Assignable-contract/title path plausible; legal/texting caveats known

hardFilters: Fewer than five active builders nearby → reject or hold
Every parcel is unique / heavy diligence → hold
Fully built-out core metros like Miami → avoid
Very rural markets without builders/buyers → avoid
Heavy compliance/texting constraints → hold until compliant workflow exists

markets.name: Lehigh Acres, FL
markets.thesis: High-volume quarter-acre infill vacant lots with repeat builder demand and standardized seller targeting.
caveats: buyer validation still required per builder

markets.name: Cape Coral, FL
markets.thesis: Visible builder demand with canal/seawall and utility-confirmed lot premiums, but diligence is more complex than clean inland infill.
caveats: flood and waterfront diligence can slow deals

markets.name: Bonita Springs, FL
markets.thesis: Southwest Florida builder demand signal, but likely more expensive and diligence-heavy than Lehigh/Cape Coral.
caveats: higher land prices may compress spread

markets.name: Jacksonville, FL
markets.thesis: Transcript cited a multi-lot Jacksonville spread; broad metro edge may have builders and repeatable pockets.

markets.name: Orlando outskirts, FL
markets.thesis: Strong Central Florida growth and builder base; needs suburb/ZIP selection outside the built-out core.

markets.name: Wilmington, NC
markets.thesis: Coastal Carolina growth market with likely scattered-lot builders; validate flood/wetlands and exact buyer pockets first.

markets.name: North Carolina growth belts
markets.thesis: Transcript mentioned NC broadly; likely target is metro-edge builder growth around defined ZIPs, not generic rural acreage.

markets.name: South Carolina growth belts
markets.thesis: Likely metro-edge new-build pockets near growing Carolina metros; good candidate for weekly research once narrowed.

markets.name: Georgia growth belts
markets.thesis: Strong Southeast growth and builder discoverability; target outer-metro ZIPs with repeatable residential lots.

markets.name: Tennessee growth belts
markets.thesis: Growth pressure around Tennessee metros can create builder demand, but terrain/slope and parcel variability need filtering.
caveats: cookie-cutter lot pattern must be proven

markets.name: Bentonville / Northwest Arkansas
markets.thesis: Strong economic growth and builder activity, but slope/perc/buildability can reduce clean repeatability.

markets.name: Houston outskirts, TX
markets.thesis: Huge builder market and outer-subdivision growth; only proceed with compliant texting/assignment/title workflow.

markets.name: Fort Worth outskirts, TX
markets.thesis: DFW edge growth may fit the transcript model if compliance/title path is locked down first.

markets.name: Austin outskirts, TX
markets.thesis: Austin-edge demand is strong but prices/competition/compliance may compress beginner spreads.

markets.name: Nevada growth belts
markets.thesis: Transcript mentions Nevada; likely requires narrowing to active new-build pockets with enough vacant residential lots.

markets.name: Arizona lot-package markets
markets.thesis: Transcript cited a 22-lot Arizona package; packageable subdivisions may work if water/utilities/builders validate.

markets.name: Washington growth belts
markets.thesis: Growth exists, but entitlement/buildability/regulatory complexity likely makes this a later-stage market.

markets.name: Hawaii growth belts
markets.thesis: Mentioned in transcript, but high complexity and island-specific constraints make it a watchlist market, not v1 execution target.
caveats: not suitable for first operating loop
